description_long: This is a small, rectangular cabin with a gable roof covered in yellowish wooden planks, giving it a rustic appearance. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray material, framed with dark wooden beams that provide structural accents. The front facade features a single arched wooden door, slightly raised with a small step, and two symmetrically placed windows with green frames and crossbars. A prominent stone chimney rises along one side of the cabin, extending above the roofline. The rear side has two additional windows, mirroring the front but without a door, while the gable ends are solid with visible timber framing. The overall design is simple and functional, evoking traditional countryside or early settler architecture.
description: A rustic, rectangular cabin with a yellow gable roof, timber-framed gray walls, green-paned windows, and a stone chimney. The front features an arched wooden door and symmetrical windows.
description_view_0: The front view shows the arched wooden door, two green-framed windows, timber accents, and the stone chimney on the left.
description_view_1: The left gable end displays the stone chimney running up the center, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals two green-framed windows symmetrically placed, with no door or chimney visible.
description_view_3: The right gable end shows only the timber-framed wall with no openings or chimney.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a moderate pitch, covered in yellow wooden planks, featuring a stone chimney on one side
attribute_window_arrangement: Four small, rectangular windows with green frames and crossbars, symmetrically arranged—two on the front facade flanking the door, and two on the rear wall, evenly spaced
attribute_door_placement: A single arched wooden door, offset to the right on the front facade between two windows
